dev:
कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन।
मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि॥
iast:
karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana
mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo 'stv akarmaṇi
eng: Your right is to act, never to the fruits; do not act for the sake of results, nor shrink into doing nothing.

dev:
योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय।
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते॥
iast:
yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṃ tyaktvā dhanañjaya
siddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṃ yoga ucyate
eng: Established in yoga, do your work without clinging; stay the same in success and failure — that evenness is yoga.

dev:
दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः।
वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थिरधीर्मुनिरुच्यते॥
iast:
duḥkheṣv anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣu vigata-spṛhaḥ
vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhaḥ sthira-dhīr munir ucyate
eng: Unshaken in pain, free of craving in pleasure, beyond passion, fear, and anger — such a sage is called steadfast in wisdom.

dev:
उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्।
आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः॥
iast:
uddhared ātmanātmānaṃ nātmānam avasādayet
ātmaiva hy ātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ
eng: Lift yourself by yourself; do not let yourself sink. The self is its own friend, and the self is its own enemy.

dev:
यतो यतो निश्चरति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम्।
तस्मात्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत्॥
iast:
yato yato niścarati manaś cañcalam asthiram
tasmāt tato niyamya etad ātmany eva vaśaṃ nayet
eng: From wherever the restless, unsteady mind wanders, from there one should draw it back and hold it under the self’s rule.

dev:
आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत्।
तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी॥
iast:
āpūryamāṇam acala-pratiṣṭhaṃ samudram āpaḥ praviśanti yadvat
tadvat kāmā yaṃ praviśanti sarve sa śāntim āpnoti na kāma-kāmī
eng: The one into whom all desires enter as waters enter the ocean, which though ever being filled remains unmoved, attains peace; not the one who longs for them.

dev:
सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।
अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥
iast:
sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṃ śaraṇaṃ vraja
ahaṃ tvā sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ
eng: Abandoning all dharmas, take refuge in Me alone; I will free you from all evil — do not grieve.

dev:
ईशा वास्यमिदꣳ सर्वं यत्किञ्च जगत्यां जगत्।
तेन त्यक्तेन भुञ्जीथा मा गृधः कस्यस्विद्धनम्॥
iast:
īśāvāsyam idaṃ sarvaṃ yat kiñca jagatyāṃ jagat
tena tyaktena bhuñjīthā mā gṛdhaḥ kasyasvid dhanam
eng: The Lord envelops all this — whatever moves in this world. Enjoy through renunciation; do not covet anyone’s wealth.

dev:
न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः।
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे॥
iast:
na jāyate mriyate vā kadācin nāyaṃ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ
ajo nityaḥ śāśvato 'yaṃ purāṇo na hanyate hanyamāne śarīre
eng: It is never born, nor does it ever die; nor, having been, does it ever cease to be. Unborn, eternal, changeless, ancient, it is not slain when the body is slain.

dev: अणोरणीयान्महतो महीयानात्मास्य जन्तोर्निहितो गुहायाम्।
iast: aṇor aṇīyān mahato mahīyān ātmāsya jantor nihito guhāyām
eng: Finer than the fine, vaster than the vast, the Self is set in the secret cave of every creature’s heart.

dev:
उत्तिष्ठत जाग्रत प्राप्य वरान्निबोधत।
क्षुरस्य धारा निशिता दुरत्यया दुर्गं पथस्तत्कवयो वदन्ति॥
iast:
uttiṣṭhata jāgrata prāpya varān nibodhata
kṣurasya dhārā niśitā duratyayā durgaṃ pathas tat kavayo vadanti
eng: Arise, awake; having approached the wise, know this. The path is sharp as a razor’s edge, hard to cross — so say the seers.

dev:
द्वा सुपर्णा सयुजा सखाया समानं वृक्षं परिषस्वजाते।
तयोरन्यः पिप्पलं स्वाद्वत्त्यनश्नन्नन्यो अभिचाकशीति॥
iast:
dvā suparṇā sayujā sakhāyā samānaṃ vṛkṣaṃ pariṣasvajāte
tayor anyaḥ pippalaṃ svādv atty anaśnann anyo 'bhicākaśīti
eng: Two birds, close companions, cling to one tree: one eats the sweet fruit, the other watches without eating.

dev:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्।
स्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेयः परधर्मो भयावहः॥
iast:
śreyān svadharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt svanuṣṭhitāt
svadharme nidhanaṃ śreyaḥ para-dharmo bhayāvahaḥ
eng: Better is one’s own duty, though imperfect, than another’s duty well performed. Better to die in one’s own duty; another’s duty is fraught with fear.

dev:
असतो मा सद्गमय।
तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय।
मृत्योर्मा अमृतं गमय।
iast:
asato mā sad gamaya
tamaso mā jyotir gamaya
mṛtyor mā amṛtaṃ gamaya
eng: Lead me from unreality to reality; from darkness to light; from death to immortality.

dev:
श्रद्धावान् लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः।
ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति॥
iast:
śraddhāvān labhate jñānaṃ tat-paraḥ saṃyatendriyaḥ
jñānaṃ labdhvā parāṃ śāntim acireṇādhigacchati
eng: The one who has faith, is devoted, and has mastered the senses gains knowledge; having gained knowledge, one soon attains supreme peace.

dev:
ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति यः।
लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा॥
iast:
brahmaṇy ādhāya karmāṇi saṅgaṃ tyaktvā karoti yaḥ
lipyate na sa pāpena padma-patram ivāmbhasā
eng: One who acts, offering all actions to Brahman and giving up attachment, is not tainted by sin, just as a lotus leaf is not tainted by water.

dev: योगश्चित्तवृत्तिनिरोधः।
iast: yogaś citta-vṛtti-nirodhaḥ
eng: Yoga is the stilling of the movements of the mind.

dev: मैत्रीकरुणामुदितोपेक्षाणां सुखदुःखपुण्यापुण्यविषयाणां भावनातश्चित्तप्रसादनम्।
iast: maitrī-karuṇā-muditopekṣāṇāṃ sukha-duḥkha-puṇya-apuṇya-viṣayāṇāṃ bhāvanātaś citta-prasādanam
eng: Clarity of mind comes from cultivating friendliness, compassion, joy, and equanimity toward the pleasant and unpleasant, the holy and the unholy.

dev:
अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते।
तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम्॥
iast:
ananyāś cintayanto māṃ ye janāḥ paryupāsate
teṣāṃ nityābhiyuktānāṃ yoga-kṣemaṃ vahāmy aham
eng: Those who think of none but Me and worship Me — to them, ever steadfast, I bring gain and guard what they have.

dev:
विद्याविनयसम्पन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि।
शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः॥
iast:
vidyā-vinaya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini
śuni caiva śva-pāke ca paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśinaḥ
eng: The wise see the same Self in a learned brāhmaṇa, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and an outcaste.

dev:
यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः।
हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः॥
iast:
yasmān nodvijate loko lokān nodvijate ca yaḥ
harṣāmarṣa-bhayodvegair mukto yaḥ sa ca me priyaḥ
eng: He by whom the world is not troubled and who is not troubled by the world, free from elation, intolerance, fear, and anxiety — he is dear to Me.

dev:
यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते।
सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथात्मा नोपलिप्यते॥
iast:
yathā sarva-gataṃ saukṣmyād ākāśaṃ nopalipyate
sarvatrāvasthito dehe tathātmā nopalipyate
eng: As all-pervading space is not tainted, because of its subtlety, so the Self, though present in every body, is not tainted.

dev:
सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च।
वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम्॥
iast:
sarvasya cāhaṃ hṛdi sanniviṣṭo mattaḥ smṛtir jñānam apohanaṃ ca
vedaiś ca sarvair aham eva vedyo vedānta-kṛd vedavid eva cāham
eng: I dwell in the heart of all; from Me come memory, knowledge, and loss thereof. I am to be known by all the Vedas; I am the author of Vedānta and the knower of the Vedas.

dev:
तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर।
असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः॥
iast:
tasmād asaktaḥ satataṃ kāryaṃ karma samācara
asakto hy ācaran karma param āpnoti pūruṣaḥ
eng: Therefore, without attachment, always do what must be done; acting without clinging, a person reaches the highest.

dev:
यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत।
अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम्॥
iast:
yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata
abhyutthānam adharmasya tad ātmānaṃ sṛjāmy aham
eng: Whenever dharma falters and adharma rises, I manifest Myself.

dev:
परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्।
धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे॥
iast:
paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṃ vināśāya ca duṣkṛtām
dharma-saṃsthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge
eng: For the rescue of the good, the ruin of evildoers, and to establish dharma, I take birth in age after age.

dev:
अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः।
अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च॥
iast:
aham ātmā guḍākeśa sarva-bhūtāśaya-sthitaḥ
aham ādiś ca madhyaṃ ca bhūtānām anta eva ca
eng: I am the Self, O Guḍākeśa, seated in the hearts of all beings; I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of beings.

dev:
सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि।
ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शनः॥
iast:
sarva-bhūta-stham ātmānaṃ sarva-bhūtāni cātmani
īkṣate yoga-yuktātmā sarvatra sama-darśanaḥ
eng: With the mind harmonized by yoga, one sees the Self abiding in all beings and all beings in the Self; one sees the same everywhere.

dev:
त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः।
कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत्॥
iast:
trividhaṃ narakasyedaṃ dvāraṃ nāśanam ātmanaḥ
kāmaḥ krodhas tathā lobhas tasmād etat trayaṃ tyajet
eng: This threefold gate of hell destroys the self — desire, anger, and greed; therefore abandon these three.

dev:
अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत्।
स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते॥
iast:
anudvega-karaṃ vākyaṃ satyaṃ priya-hitaṃ ca yat
svādhyāyābhyasanaṃ caiva vāṅmayaṃ tapa ucyate
eng: Speech that causes no distress, is true, kind, and helpful, together with recitation of scripture — that is called austerity of speech.

dev:
यत्करोषि यदश्नासि यज्जुहोषि ददासि यत्।
यततपस्यसि कौन्तेय तत्कुरुष्व मदर्पणम्॥
iast:
yat karoṣi yad aśnāsi yaj juhoṣi dadāsi yat
yat tapasyasi kaunteya tat kuruṣva mad-arpaṇam
eng: Whatever you do, eat, offer, or give, whatever austerity you perform — do it, O Kaunteya, as an offering to Me.

dev:
मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः।
आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत॥
iast:
mātra-sparśās tu kaunteya śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ
āgamāpāyino 'nityās tāṃs titikṣasva bhārata
eng: The contacts of sense and object, O Kaunteya, bring cold and heat, pleasure and pain; they come and go and do not last — endure them, Bhārata.
